adrRules.title: Spatial logic only via the PathfinderService interface
adrRules.msg: calls WasmPathfinderService directly (use the PathfinderService interface)

adrRules.reason: Layered singletons — layer direction is enforced by the `layers` rule; "import the singleton, never new XImpl()" needs new-expression analysis.
GameStateManager-only mutation — needs field-assignment analysis, not call edges.
All stat calcs via ModifierSystem — a semantic contract, not a structural edge.
AI generation server-side only — a deployment/runtime boundary.
LocalStorage persistence via store — runtime behaviour.
Data files = definitions not logic — partly covered by the `layers` rule (core must not call services).
Tech-stack choice (SvelteKit + WebGL2) — not an invariant.
Production-chain game design — content, not structure.
Need-priority interruption formula — runtime behaviour.
Gated hot-path logging — runtime behaviour.
Combat wound model — game design.
Deferred combat depth — game design.
Tile occupancy via OccupancyService — bypass detection needs field-level analysis.
Single work model — the superseded path was deleted; nothing to detect.
Physical production (reserve-and-fetch) — runtime behaviour.

Data-driven jobs (jobs.jsonc <-> Job[type] union <-> JobService handler registry) — a data-coverage invariant, not a call edge. Drift is enforced by jobRegistry.test.ts + compile-time JobPoolType guards.
Perception via target persistence + push alerting — runtime AI behaviour and per-entity state, not a call edge.
Line of sight via blocksSight occluder + WASM raycast — data-schema + runtime; the 'callsites never import spatial-core directly' part is already covered by the ADR-008 forbidden-callee-module rule.
Sim scaling strategy (algorithms->WASM->worker, defer multicore) — a sequencing/runtime decision, not a structural invariant.
Sim/render decouple (soft-body pathfinding, terrain cache, MAX_STEPS cap, sim->Worker) — runtime/perf behaviour, not a call-edge invariant.
Throttled job-board reconcile (6-tick cadence, not event-driven) — a runtime cadence/perf decision, not a structural call-edge invariant.

Procedural race pool + pawn raceId (racePool canonical, race = home alias; mixed colonies; trait-resistance condition hook; raceRelations stub) — a data-model + runtime decision, not a structural call-edge invariant. Generator drift is guarded by Race.test.ts.

Data-driven body plans (limbmap.jsonc) + part-bound natural weapons & armour + bodyScale-scaled limb HP + fractures — a data-schema + runtime decision, not a structural call-edge invariant. Drift is guarded by bodyPlans.test.ts + fractures.test.ts.

Graded wind → staged windchilled condition (direct-set), windchill cold coupling, 8-way windDir, and per-pawn upwind ray-march wind-shadow (chosen over a precomputed field) — a data + runtime/perf decision, not a structural call-edge invariant. Guarded by windchill.test.ts.

adrRules.title: Full-map terrain build only via the terrainPaint seam
adrRules.msg: full-map terrain rebuild outside the terrainPaint seam (ADR-026: the per-delta path must repaint only changed tiles)

adrRules.title: Whole-map hidden-mask BFS only via the terrainPaint seam
adrRules.msg: whole-map hidden-mask BFS outside the terrainPaint seam (ADR-026: use updateHiddenMaskAt incrementally on a delta)

adrRules.reason: Dense glyph overlays (resource short/tall) render via the cached chunk path, not the per-frame dynamic path — a runtime ARGUMENT property (renderGrid always holds both branches; the cached one is selected by passing chunkLayer/cacheVersion), so a regression drops an argument without changing any call edge. Same class as ADR-021's terrain cache. Flagged instead by the resourceRebuilds= counter in perf.log (spikes every frame if the cache breaks).

Typed trait kinds (stat/attribute strictly separated + naming law; bodyMod reshapes the limbmap; naturalGear armor→encumbrance + limb-bound weapons via hostParts; wounds-as-permanent-injuries; a negative FLAW tier drawn as a Gaussian count) and the condition relationship graph (flags/triggers/activateWhen; chance-less edges stay deterministic) — data-schema + payload-shape + RNG-distribution invariants, not call-edge ones. Guarded by traitRegistry.test.ts, traitFlaws.test.ts, conditionGraph(.Data).test.ts, traitWounds.test.ts.
